title: user — subprocessors
description: The user module itself does not engage third-party processors directly — every field is stored in the tenant's own database. Adjacent processors that carry User-scoped data are engaged by sibling modules. This blueprint documents them for the compliance ledger.

subprocessors.name: AWS S3
subprocessors.purpose: Persist `Profile.avatar_url` uploads. One object per avatar, keyed by profile_id + upload nonce.
subprocessors.location: customer's AWS region (tenant-selectable at provisioning; defaults to the tenant's region.data_residency_zone)
subprocessors.when_engaged: user.profile.allow_avatar_upload = true AND caller successfully calls POST /me/avatar

subprocessors.name: Postmark
data_processed: Template variables (tenant name, actor name, reactivation date)
subprocessors.when_engaged: any lifecycle notification with mail in its channel list

subprocessors.name: Twilio
subprocessors.purpose: Optional SMS notification when the tenant's notifications config routes user-lifecycle events to the `sms` channel. Rare — most tenants keep this off.
subprocessors.when_engaged: notification category default_channels contains 'sms' AND profile->phone_e164 is set

notes: AWS S3 avatar bytes are subject to EU data residency when the tenant's region is EU — the media module reads `region.data_residency_zone` at bucket resolution time.

The user module does NOT engage OpenAI / Google / DeepL / any translation provider directly — Profile.locale is a string tag, never machine-translated. Localised UI strings routed through localization::TranslatorDriverManager reference translation subprocessors under that module's ledger.

Postmark + Twilio DPAs are signed by the platform (Stackra). Tenant-level opt-out is available for `functional_optional` notifications only; `transactional_required` notifications cannot be opted out of per GDPR Art. 6(1)(b) (contract performance).
